name: Black-Bottom Strawberry Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly grease a 9-inch pie plate.
2. Process the shortbread cookies and packed brown sugar in a food processor until finely crushed and blended. Add the melted butter and pulse just until crumbs are moistened.
3. Press the mixture evenly and firmly over the bottom and sides of the pie plate. Place it in the freezer to firm up the crust.
4. Heat heavy cream (or chocolate) in a small saucepan or microwave just until steaming hot. Remove from heat and add chocolate chips. Let stand 1-2 minutes, then stir until the chocolate melts and the mixture is thick and well blended. Spread evenly over the bottom of the pie crust. Refrigerate or freeze to firm up the chocolate layer.
5. In a medium saucepan, mix granulated sugar and cornstarch. Stir in water, strawberry jam, and lemon juice. Add hulled strawberries (save the prettiest ones for the top) and mash with a potato masher. Bring to a gentle boil over medium heat, whisking constantly. Boil 6-7 minutes until thick and syrupy. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
6. Drain remaining berries on paper towels, patting dry. Pour about one-third of the glaze over the chocolate layer, spreading with a pastry brush. Top with strawberries, points up. Pour remaining glaze over the berries, coating each berry. Refrigerate at least 1 hour to set the glaze.
Per serving: 449 cal, 3 g pro, 69 g carb, 20 g fat
